summary:

Ingredients:

1 cup all-purpose flour

1 cup brown sugar

1/2 cup granulated sugar

1/2 cup butter, softened

1 egg

1 teaspoon vanilla extract

1 cup semisweet chocolate chips

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).

2. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

3. In a large bowl, combine the flour, brown sugar, granulated sugar, butter, egg, and vanilla extract.

4. Stir until well combined.

5. Fold in the chocolate chips.

6. Drop the dough by rounded tablespoons onto the prepared baking sheet.

7. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set.

8. Let cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely. | I like to spend time with my family, and I enjoy playing recreational baseball. summary:
I have a lot of favorite jokes, but one of my favorites is:
"A guy walks into a bar and orders a drink. As he sits there, he hears a high-pitched voice say, "Hey, those jeans look really great on you!"
The man looks around but sees nothing. He then returns to his drink thinking nothing more of it. But then, a moment later, he hears the same voice again, "I really like what you have done with your hair!"
The man again glances around but sees nothing. Now he wonders if should visit a doctor as he clearly seems to be hallucinating. He calmes himself down but then as he believes the voice is gone, he hears, "You seem like an awesome person!"
He puts his drink down, completely scared and looks around wildly. Still unable to find the source of the voice, he calls over to the bartender, "Hey! What's that voice I keep hearing?"
"Those are the peanuts," the bartender replies. "They're complimentary."" | That's a great idea! I love couponing too. Instructions: 1. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, eggs, olive oil, and salt.
2. Mix until the ingredients are well combined and a dough forms.

4. Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
5. After the dough has rested, cut it in half and roll out each half into a thin sheet.

salient_points: So, overall it looks pretty good. I like the format and the way you've organized your information.

There are a few things I would recommend changing, though. First, I would add a summary statement at the top of your resume. This is a great way to highlight your skills and experience, and to give potential employers a quick overview of what you're all about.

Second, I would try to quantify your experience whenever possible. For example, instead of just saying that you "managed a team of employees," say that you "managed a team of 10 employees and increased sales by 15%." This will help potential employers see the value you can bring to their organization.
Finally, I would proofread your resume carefully and make sure there are no errors. This is a small detail, but it can make a big difference.

summary:
Thanks! I really like it so far. I'm actually getting ready to start my clinical rotations soon. | Those are great choices! I've read a few books by Stephen King, and they're always really good. I've also read some biographies, but I haven't read much historical fiction. | That's so cool!  I love The Walking Dead.  I'm actually in the middle of writing a short story about it.  Do you want to read it? | Ok, here it goes:

* * *

The zombies were coming.  I could hear them shambling towards me, their moans echoing in the night.  I knew I had to run, but my legs wouldn't work.  I was frozen with fear.

Suddenly, I heard a voice.  It was a familiar voice.  It was the voice of my friend, Rick.

"Don't give up!" he shouted.  "Run!"

I took a deep breath and started to run.  I ran as fast as I could, but the zombies were gaining on me.  I could feel their hot breath on the back of my neck.

Just when I thought I was going to be caught, I saw a light in the distance.  It was a house!  I ran towards it, and the zombies followed me.

I slammed the door behind me and locked it.  The zombies were trapped outside.  I was safe.

I collapsed on the floor, exhausted.  I had survived.  But how long would I last?  The zombies were getting closer.

I looked around the room for something to use as a weapon.  I saw a baseball bat in the corner.  I picked it up and held it tightly in my hands.

The zombies started to bang on the door.  I could hear them growling and snarling.  They wanted to get in.

I took a deep breath and prepared to fight.  The door burst open, and the zombies came flooding in.

I swung my bat as hard as I could, and the zombies started to fall.  I fought for my life, and I won.

I was the only survivor.

* * *

What did you think?

Ingredients:

2 cups all-purpose flour

1 teaspoon salt

1/2 cup water

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt.

2. Add the water and mix until the dough comes together and forms a ball.

3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes, until it is smooth and elastic.

4. Place the dough in a greased bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let the dough rest for at least 30 minutes, or up to overnight.

5. After the dough has rested, divide it into 4 equal pieces. Roll out each piece of dough into a thin sheet, about 1/8 inch thick.

6. Cut the dough into your desired shape and size.

7. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.

8. Add the pasta and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until al dente.

9. Drain the pasta and serve immediately with your favorite sauce.

summary:
Sure! Here you go:
Ingredients:
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
- 1/4 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 large egg
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon vegetable oil
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 1 cup finely chopped watermelon
Directions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg, milk, vegetable oil, and vanilla extract.
4. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Fold in the watermelon.
5. Drop the dough by rounded tablespoons onto the prepared baking sheet.
6. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set.
7. Let cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely. | Oh yeah, he's so great! Have you seen his movies Raging Bull or Taxi Driver? | I've read a few books by Stephen King, but I haven't read any historical fiction or biographies. I'll have to check them out! | I'm so excited for my cosplay costume to come in the mail! I'm going to start practicing my makeup this weekend.

summary:
Hi Brad, my name is Sarah. I love animals too. I used to take my cats to the farm and let them play with the pigs, horses and hens. | Hi! I'm a high school student who is worried about my future. I'm looking forward to starting a new life in a new state with my dog! | I totally get that! Making friends in a new place can be really hard. I've found that it's helpful to join clubs or groups that are interested in the same things you are. That way you can meet people who you already have something in common with. And as for finding a job, there are tons of resources available to help you out. You can check out your local library or career center, or you can search online for job listings. | Sure! Here it is:

1 cup chopped onions
1 cup chopped celery
1/2 cup chopped carrots
2 tablespoons chopped garlic
1/4 cup butter
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour
4 cups chicken broth
1 cup dry white wine
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon dried thyme
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1 1/2 pounds lobster meat, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 cup heavy cream
1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley

Instructions:

In a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat, saute the onions, celery, carrots, and garlic in the butter until softened.
Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, wine, salt, thyme, black pepper, and cayenne pepper and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Add the lobster meat and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the cream and parsley and cook until heated through.
Serve immediately.

summary:
Sure, here it is:

Ingredients:
- 1 pound of boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
- 1 tablespoon of olive oil
- 1 tablespoon of minced garlic
- 1 teaspoon of grated fresh ginger
- 1/2 cup of chopped onion
- 1/2 cup of chopped bell pepper
- 1/2 cup of chopped carrots
- 1 tablespoon of soy sauce
- 1 tablespoon of oyster sauce
- 1 tablespoon of hoisin sauce
- 1/4 cup of water
- 1/4 cup of cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons of cold water

Instructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides.
3. Add the garlic, ginger, onion, bell pepper, and carrots and cook until softened.
4. Stir in the soy sauce, oyster sauce, hoisin sauce, and water.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and cold water until smooth.
6. Add the cornstarch mixture to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.
7. Serve over rice. | I'm a creative person who loves to express myself through music, drawing, and painting. I'm also single and live with my three cats. | I love to collect music and other things that represent my personality and interests. I also like to go to concerts and art shows. | It's definitely not for everyone, but I think they're a great way to see different types of art and meet new people.

summary:
Oh, that's great! I love to cook. I have a few tips for you. First, don't be afraid to experiment. Try new recipes and don't be afraid to make mistakes. Second, don't be afraid to use spices. Spices can really make a dish come alive. Third, cook with fresh ingredients whenever possible. This will make your food taste better and be healthier. | I love to make pasta dishes, especially lasagna. I also love to make soups and stews. I'm not a big fan of baking, but I do like to make cookies sometimes. | No problem! Here's the recipe:

Ingredients:

1 pound lasagna noodles
1 pound ground beef
1 pound Italian sausage
1 (28 ounce) can crushed tomatoes
2 (15 ounce) cans tomato sauce
1 (10 ounce) can tomato soup
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
1/2 cup ricotta cheese
1/2 cup grated mozzarella cheese
1/2 cup chopped onion
1/2 cup chopped green bell pepper
1/2 cup chopped red bell pepper
1/4 cup chopped fresh basil
1/4 teaspoon dried oregano
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/8 teaspoon black pepper

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
2. In a large skillet, brown the ground beef and Italian sausage. Drain excess fat.
3. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, tomato sauce, tomato soup, Parmesan cheese, ricotta cheese, mozzarella cheese, onion, green bell pepper, red bell pepper, basil, oregano, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
4. In a 9x13 inch baking dish, spread 1/3 of the sauce mixture. Top with a layer of lasagna noodles. Repeat layers, ending with a layer of sauce.
5. Cover the lasagna with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for an additional 20 minutes, or until the noodles are cooked through and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
6. Let cool for 10 minutes before serving. | I mainly play action and adventure games. I'm really excited for the new God of War game coming out next year.

summary:
Here's the recipe I use:

Ingredients:

1 pound artichoke hearts
1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes, chopped
1 cup pesto sauce
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
1/4 cup olive oil
1/4 cup chopped fresh basil
1 pound penne pasta

Instructions:

1. In a large bowl, combine the artichoke hearts, sun-dried tomatoes, pesto sauce, Parmesan cheese, olive oil, and basil.
2. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta according to the package directions.
3. Drain the pasta and add it to the bowl with the artichoke mixture. Toss to combine.
4. Serve immediately. | I also like to volunteer at my local animal shelter.
